title: Published `glimpse` bin depends on Bun without declaring that runtime contract
reasoning: The package exposes `glimpse` as an npm bin, but that bin is the raw TypeScript source file with a `#!/usr/bin/env bun` shebang. Consumers invoking the installed command need a `bun` executable on PATH; otherwise the entrypoint fails before the CLI code runs. The manifest does not declare an `engines` requirement, fallback Node build, or release artifact that would make this contract explicit.
reproduction: Install the package on a machine without Bun and invoke `glimpse`; `/usr/bin/env` fails to locate `bun` before any CLI logic executes.
recommendation: Either publish a compiled JavaScript bin runnable under Node, or explicitly declare Bun as a runtime requirement via package metadata and installation/docs so consumers do not get a broken executable unexpectedly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No packaging or install smoke tests are linked, so there is nothing verifying the published bin works in a clean environment.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a release smoke test that packs/installs the package and verifies the installed `glimpse` executable starts successfully in the supported runtime, plus a metadata check that the required runtime is declared.
